content: make a report on rise of LLM

The trajectory of natural language processing reveals a profound shift from theoretical frameworks to ubiquitous infrastructural deployment. While early conceptualizations of machine translation date back to seventeenth-century philosophers [1179] and mid-1930s mechanical proposals utilizing paper tape [1026, 1332], modern Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved unprecedented scale through a deceptively simple mechanism.
